title: EDR auth + authz
description: Operator dashboard for the wave-1 auth surface, scoped to signals that actually populate in SigNoz today. Two panels: HTTP-trace-based authz deny rate (spans whose response status is 403, the chokepoint deny shape), and audit-write failures (slog WARNs the async writer emits on drops + INSERT errors). The wave-1 audit pipeline writes every successful row to MySQL only; it does not dual-emit to the OTel log stream on success. Panels for OIDC + break-glass login outcomes and bootstrap-token issuance need slog dual-emit on success first (a wave-2 deliverable); querying audit_events directly in MySQL is the only reliable source until then. SigNoz's import dialog re-numbers panels; the layout block is a starting hint only.

panels.title: Authz deny rate by handler
panels.description: Count of HTTP request spans whose response status is 403, grouped by route. The chokepoint returns 403 (with header X-Edr-Authz-Reason) on every deny verdict, so this panel is a faithful proxy for chokepoint deny rate without requiring slog dual-emit on the audit row. A sudden spike on a single handler usually means a recent role-binding change; a flat baseline of low-volume denies on /api/host/.../isolate (etc.) means the freshness gate is firing as designed. The deny reason itself is not in OTel today: cross-reference audit_events.payload.reason in MySQL when investigating a specific spike.

panels.title: Audit-write failures (drops + INSERT errors)
panels.description: The async audit writer emits a slog WARN when it cannot enqueue a row (queue_full / writer_stopped) or when the global drain deadline expires (drain_deadline_exceeded), and a separate WARN when an INSERT into audit_events fails. Any non-zero rate here is a hard alert: the audit log is the spec's append-only invariant and dropping rows breaks it. Drops carry a 'reason' attribute; INSERT failures carry an 'err' attribute instead; the body string distinguishes the two cases. The query below filters on the body regex and groups by body so the panel splits drop vs INSERT-failure cleanly.
